div.p: Three parallel arrays with byte offsets, element sizes, and cache line boundaries

ul.li: SOA (Structure of Arrays): Dense and Entities arrays are parallel.
Indirect Mapping: Sparse array provides stable indices for entities.
Memory Footprint: sizeof(usize) * max_entity_id + (sizeof(T) + sizeof(u32)) * count.
Cache Boundary: Red dashed labels indicate 64B cache alignment logic.

div.p: Sparse Array Maps entity index → dense index Element size: 8 bytes (usize on 64-bit) Sentinel: EMPTY = usize::MAX
📦 Cache Line (64 bytes) 8 elements per line [0..7] fits in 1 cache line
Dense Array Packed components (no gaps) Example: Position { x: f32, y: f32 } = 8 bytes
📦 Cache Line (64 bytes) 64 bytes ÷ 8 bytes = 8 entities Maximizes prefetcher efficiency
Entities Array Parallel to dense array Size: 4 bytes (Entity = u32)
📦 Cache Line (64 bytes) 16 Entity IDs per line
